mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.4% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 5.2% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity valuations and forward earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1047k units, RSAFS at 465914M
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST declining > 3% MoM), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.0 signals supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO < 104.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k persons and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive models over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in real GDP (GDPC1) above 16800 billion chained 2017 dollars signals robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16702.1, below expansion threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 16800 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 242.0) signals monetary policy lag that creates financial repression and equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as real rates remain negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between low FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 4-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor contraction that reduces aggregate productive capacity and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% and PAYEMS declines > 50k persons in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO decline and PAYEMS decline is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive models over 4-year rolling windows.
